---
title: "Mrs Reeves"
---

## Mrs Reeves

**Dates:** (fl.22 July 1845)

**Maiden name:** (Mason)

### Description

schoolmistress

### Biography

Probably Charlotte Mason, CMY’s former nurse, sister of the transported agitator Joseph Mason (1799-1863). She could be the Charlotte Reeves (1803/4-1871) who kept a lodging-house at Ventnor, IOW, but that person seems already to have been there in 1841. She could also be the ‘old nurse of mine who married a drum major’ mentioned in a letter to Ann Carter Smith (31 March 1864). Coleridge, <i>Life</i>, 80-1; <i>John Keble’s Parishes</i>, 96-7; Charlotte M. Yonge, <i>An Old Woman’s Outlook in a Hampshire Village</i> (London: Macmillan: 1892), 65-7; <i>Joseph Mason: Assigned Convict 1831</i>-1837ed. David Kent and Norma Townsend (Melbourne: Melbourne University Press 1996), 3.

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
